title: Biblical Sense
headline: Biblical Sense is a reference worth keeping
caption:
Biblical Sense works best when the reference lands immediately and needs no explanation. A double-entendre joke on 'knowing someone in the biblical sense,' which can mean having sex with them; applying that phrase to Jesus sounds innocent at first because he appears throughout the Bible, but the alternate meaning makes it absurdly inappropriate. It is a strong fit for people who like irreverent religious humor, double entendre fans, fans of taboo wordplay, especially if A double-entendre joke on 'knowing someone in the biblical sense,' which can mean having sex with them; applying that phrase to Jesus sounds innocent at first because he appears throughout the Bible, but the alternate meaning makes it absurdly inappropriate. is already familiar. https://www.thirdstringshirts.com/shop.html#!/biblical+sense?idea=5d8e1581b4b8c76220050334 Reply if you get the reference.

title: I Put The B In Subtle
headline: I Put The B In Subtle earns a second look
caption:
I Put The B In Subtle is specific enough to stop the scroll even before the joke fully registers. A wordplay joke on the bragging format 'I put the X in Y,' using the silent B in 'subtle' to boast about being subtle even though the letter itself is already hidden. It is the kind of design that rewards curiosity and a slightly strange sense of humor. https://www.thirdstringshirts.com/shop.html#!/i+put+the+b+in+subtle?idea=5d89cc026bbdbb2e6a46ab51 Reply if you get the reference.

title: I Can't Feel My Perineum
headline: I Can't Feel My Perineum is built for committed fans
caption:
I Can't Feel My Perineum leans into sports without pretending to be for casual observers. A cycling joke about how long rides and bike seats can cause numbness in the perineum. This one is better for people who already picked a side. https://www.thirdstringshirts.com/shop.html#!/i+can't+feel+my+perineum?idea=5d89cc11b4b8c76e39170242 Reply if you get the reference.
